sanskrit:
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः।
धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः॥
transliteration:
yotsyamānān avekṣe ’haṃ ya ete ’tra samāgatāḥ
dhārtarāṣṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priya-cikīrṣavaḥ
literal_meaning: I wish to see those who have assembled here, eager to fight, desiring to do what is dear to the evil-minded son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s request to survey the battlefield reveals a moment of anticipatory appraisal—he is simultaneously assessing external threats and internal alignment before irreversible action. Psychologically, this is the calm before the storm, where curiosity, duty, and latent dread intermingle, forcing the warrior to confront the faces of those he must soon either kill or be killed by.
psychological_state: A person poised at the threshold of a life-altering decision, scanning the field of consequence while secretly measuring the cost to their own humanity.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon reviewing the pre-op scans of a beloved mentor who is now the patient, knowing the procedure could save or end the very life that shaped her own.
anchor_text: Look your choices in the eye before you loose the arrow.
